title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string s containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid. An input string is valid if: open brackets must be closed by the same type of brackets, open brackets must be closed in the correct order, and every close bracket has a corresponding open bracket of the same type.
thinking_questions: When you see a closing bracket, what do you need to know to check if it's valid?
You need to match the most recently seen unmatched opening bracket — what data structure gives you the most recent?
What are the three failure conditions?

hints.L1: When you encounter a closing bracket, you need to match it with the most recently unmatched opening bracket — think about which data structure gives you 'most recent'.
hints.L2: Stack. Push opening brackets. On closing bracket, pop from stack and check if it matches. Three failure cases: mismatch, stack empty on close, stack non-empty at end.

follow_up_questions: What are the exactly three ways this can fail?
What if the string had only one type of bracket? How would your approach simplify?
How would you handle wildcards where '*' can be '(', ')' or empty? (LC 678)
solution_notes: Stack: push opens, pop on close and verify match. Three failure modes: mismatch, empty stack on close, non-empty at end.
